title: Isolated decoder output-projection producer synthesis probe
hypothesis: The whole npu_top output-projection producer synth probe timed out at nm2 because it includes control, queue, SRAM, DMA, and AXI shell logic around the generated GEMM compute array. Synthesizing the generated gemm_compute_array module directly should reveal whether the immediate blocker is the producer arithmetic array itself or top-level integration.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Can the isolated generated gemm_compute_array producer submodule complete synth-only compilation for nm1 and nm2 within the evaluator budget?
include: nm1 and nm2 fp16 rtlgen producer configs
generated gemm_compute_array as the OpenROAD top module
same rtlgen binary setup check used by the prior producer synth-boundary probe
exclude: nm3 or larger retries before the isolated nm1/nm2 boundary is known

expected_benefit: separates arithmetic-array synthesis complexity from top-level producer integration complexity
establishes an nm1 synth anchor for the generated producer array
determines whether nm2 needs arithmetic hierarchy changes or only top-level wrapper restructuring
risks: isolated synth completion does not prove whole producer integration is feasible
clockless synth-only timing is not final PPA
the timeout boundary remains evaluator-machine dependent

required_evaluations.objective: Run a bounded synth-only probe for the isolated generated gemm_compute_array producer submodule over nm1 and nm2.

expected_result.reason: The output should distinguish whether the nm2 timeout is caused by the generated producer arithmetic array or by whole npu_top integration around it.
